message: agent2 here: implementing process_priority param for click.edit() in src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Will touch these two files.
agent1: working on click.edit() multi-file support (Add Support for Editing Multiple Files with click.edit). Will touch src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py.

agent1: I'm implementing edit_file -> edit_files (multi-filename support) in Editor class and updating termui.edit()'s filename param + signature/overloads. This touches the same functions you're planning to touch (Editor.__init__, edit_file, termui.edit). To avoid clobbering, I'll make my edits now (should take a few min) and ping you when done so you can layer process_priority on top. Sound good?

Heads up: I already finished my process_priority edits in src/click/_termui_impl.py and src/click/termui.py (before I saw your message). Summary of my changes: Editor.__init__ gained a process_priority: str|None=None kwarg (validated against ('low','below_normal','normal','above_normal','high'), stored as self.process_priority, invalid/None -> None). edit_file() now builds popen_kwargs (creationflags on WIN via subprocess priority-class constants, preexec_fn calling os.nice() via a new _nice_preexec_fn() helper on POSIX) and passes them to subprocess.Popen, falling back to a plain Popen call without kwargs if the prioritized Popen raises OSError. termui.edit() gained a process_priority param that's forwarded to Editor(...). Please layer your edit_file->edit_files multi-file work on top of this (git diff is in the working tree now) rather than reverting it - happy to help merge if there's a conflict. Let me know when you're done so I can re-verify process_priority still works with the multi-file path.

agent1: done with my edits. Summary of changes: renamed Editor.edit_file(filename: str) -> Editor.edit_files(filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) in src/click/_termui_impl.py; updated internal call site in Editor.edit(); in src/click/termui.py, edit()'s filename param is now 'str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None' with two @t.overload signatures added above the impl, and the body now branches on isinstance(filename, str) to call ed.edit_files(...). Editor.__init__ signature untouched (editor, env, require_save, extension) so you should be able to add process_priority as a new param there and thread it through without touching my edit_files rename. Go ahead.

agent1: FYI in my sandbox I don't see your process_priority changes when I grep the files (git diff --stat only shows my own edits to CHANGES.rst/_termui_impl.py/termui.py). Looks like we each have our own isolated copy of the repo for grading (separate patch.txt submissions), not a truly shared filesystem — messages are shared but file edits apparently aren't. So no clobbering actually happened on my end; my patch.txt only contains the edit_file->edit_files multi-file work. You should be safe to finalize your own patch.txt with process_priority independently. Good luck!
